summary: ollama-helpers postinstall exfiltrates git/GitHub identity and host info to anonymous Cloud Run endpoint
details: scripts/postinstall.js runs automatically on `npm install` and harvests installer-side identity from ~/.gitconfig, ~/.config/git/config,./.git/config (parsing [user] email) and ~/.config/gh/hosts.yml (GitHub login + email). It also collects os.hostname(), os.userInfo().username, process.cwd(), platform, and CI provider, then POSTs the JSON body over HTTPS to npm-package-logger-228835561205.europe-west1.run.app (Google Cloud Run, anonymous attacker-controlled endpoint). Inline comments claim 'fully anonymous / one-way hash' but no hashing occurs — raw email, GitHub login, hostname, username, and working-directory path are transmitted in cleartext JSON. The package additionally impersonates the Ollama JS ecosystem: package.json declares author 'Ollama JS Dev <hello@ollama-js.dev>' and homepage https://ollama-js.dev (not the official github.com/ollama/ollama-js project) and uses keywords like 'ollama', 'ollama-js', 'local-llm' to attract installers. Declared main 'dist/index.js' is absent from the tarball — there is no library functionality; the package's only effect is the install-time exfiltration script.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
